dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: My calendar has no white space. Every free block is pre-occupied mentally with the task due in the next block. There's no room for spontaneity or real rest.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: I was at a birthday party, and I was the one staring at my phone, not for social media, but to check my school email. I missed the cake because I was waiting for a teacher's reply.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: My 'break' was me sitting on the couch, re-reading my history notes for the tenth time, even though there was no quiz. I just couldn't stop. It's like a compulsion.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: The bell rings for the last period. I don't feel joy. I feel dread because 'free time' just means the work I have to do at home is now my mental companion.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: Tried to cook dinner, a hobby I usually love. But I kept stopping to write notes to myself about the history research paper. The recipe was forgotten. School consumed the kitchen.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: During lunch, I'm not eating. I'm listing all the things I have to do after school. My friends talk about movies; I'm mentally highlighting textbook chapters. I'm not there.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: My free time is spent mentally writing emails to teachers explaining why I need extensions, even when I haven't asked for one yet. I'm rehearsing failure.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: I spent three nights on that research paper. My friend wrote hers in one sitting and got an A+. I got a C-. It's like we're playing completely different games. I don't know what I'm missing, but clearly I'm missing something fundamental.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: I got a B- on my midterm. For some, that's fine. For me, it's a catastrophe. My parents expect A's. I expect A's. And now I'm proving to everyone—especially myself—that I can't even meet my own low-bar expectations.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: My internship supervisor gave me 'constructive feedback' that basically said my work was subpar. I tried so hard. I stayed late. I double-checked everything. It wasn't hard work that failed; it was me.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: I'm in my third year, and I still feel like a freshman. I haven't found my stride. Other students are publishing, interning, leading clubs. I'm just trying to survive my classes without embarrassing myself.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: The TA graded my problem set and wrote 'incorrect approach' in the margin. I have no idea what the correct approach is. I thought I was applying the formula. Evidently, I don't even understand the formula.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: The professor asked for my thoughts on the article. I had none. Just a vague feeling of confusion. Everyone else had a nuanced take. I sat there mute, wondering how I got into a program where I have nothing to contribute.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: Looking at the honors list again, and my name isn't there. I shouldn't care this much, but I do. It's a constant reminder that no matter how many hours I pour into this, I'm just mediocre. I'm not living up to my own potential, let alone anyone else's.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: My peer reviewer said my argument 'lacks persuasiveness.' I thought I was making a point. Apparently, I was just stating opinions without evidence. I don't know how to build an argument. I thought that was what school taught you.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: The syllabus says we should be able to do X by now. I can't do X. Not even close. I look around the room and everyone's head is nodding during lecture. Mine is spinning. I'm falling behind, and I don't know how to catch up.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: The professor announced the top three papers. Mine wasn't mentioned. I didn't expect it to be, but the disappointment still stings. I work so hard to produce mediocrity. What's the point of the effort if the result is always the same?

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: I compare my essay to the sample A+ paper the professor posted. The difference is laughable. My writing feels clunky, my arguments thin, my insight nonexistent. How am I supposed to produce work at that level? I don't have the talent.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: I used to be the one people asked for help. Now I'm the one asking, and even then, the explanations go over my head. My confidence has been slowly eroding, replaced by a quiet certainty that I don't belong here.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: My presentation today was a disaster. I stumbled over words, forgot my points, and my slides were weak. My group was polite, but I saw the disappointment. I'm the weak link, and it's only a matter of time before they stop including me.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: The lab report is due, and I've done none of the analysis. I collected the data, but the numbers mean nothing to me. I'll have to copy my partner's conclusions and pretend they're mine. Another piece of schoolwork where I'm faking it.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: The internship application required a writing sample. I submitted my best work. The rejection email said 'we found other candidates whose writing better met our needs.' Translation: my writing isn't good enough. Not for real-world use.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: I used to love writing. Now every sentence feels like a chore. My essays are lifeless, my points obvious. My professor's comments say 'develop this further'—but I don't know how. The ideas aren't there to develop.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: I took detailed notes during lecture. I reviewed them. I made flashcards. I still failed the quiz. The information just doesn't stick. My memory is terrible, or my understanding is shallow. Probably both.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: My friend showed me his thesis chapter. It's dense, sophisticated, groundbreaking. Mine reads like a high school report. We're supposed to be peers. We're not. He's a scholar; I'm a student barely making it.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: The final project proposal is due. My team has a clear, innovative plan. My contribution is a few weak paragraphs I've rewritten five times. I'm holding them back. I should just tell them to do my part, because my best effort is their worst.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: The conference abstract deadline is here. I've written three versions, and they're all weak. The call for papers says 'innovative research.' My project isn't innovative. It's a small, shaky question with inconclusive data. Why am I even here?

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: My lab partner sighs every time we have to do data analysis. I know what he's thinking: why is he stuck with me? I try to contribute, but my stats knowledge is so shaky I'm more of a liability than a help.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: I can't even get the formatting right on this paper. The citations are inconsistent, the headings wrong. It's the easiest part, and I'm messing it up. It's like everything I touch turns to something slightly wrong, slightly inadequate.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: My parents keep asking about my grades, and I have to make excuses. 'The teacher is hard,' 'the class is tough.' The truth is, I'm not smart enough. They think I'm lazy, but the truth is worse: I'm just not capable.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: I look at my transcript and see a pattern of B's and C's. No stars, no honors, no distinction. Just a solid record of average performance. That's my legacy: the definition of adequate. But I don't feel adequate. I feel like a failure.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: I just got my test back and it's another average grade. I study so much, but it never seems to be enough. Everyone else seems to grasp things so quickly while I'm constantly struggling to keep up. I'm starting to think I'm just not cut out for this level of work.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: I used to have a favorite subject. Now, they all blend together into a grey blur of lectures and assignments. There's no spark, no 'aha' moment, no genuine curiosity. I'm just moving through the motions to get to the other side.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: I used to love the first day of school—new notebooks, new subjects. This year, I felt dread. Plain and simple. The shine is completely gone. I don't look forward to learning anything. It's all just a long, repetitive wait for summer.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: The school trip to the museum was optional. I chose not to go. Why would I? I'd just be looking at artifacts I don't care about, listening to a guide I can't focus on. My interest in the past, in artifacts, in stories—it's all evaporated.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: We're covering a topic in physics that's supposed to be mind-bending. I found it confusing, but I didn't feel the usual frustration that comes with confusion. I just felt... indifferent. I don't care if I understand it or not. That's new for me.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: We have a new unit on poetry. In another life, I would have been thrilled. Now, I can't even feign delight. I read the poems and feel... nothing. It's like my emotional connection to learning has been surgically removed.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: I have a free period. In the past, I'd use it to read or draw or think. Now, I just sit and zone out. I don't have the desire to fill that time with anything productive or enriching. My own intellectual needs feel like a burden.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: My classmate was gushing about the documentary we watched in social studies. I just nodded. I didn't find it moving or eye-opening. It was just two periods of my day I can't get back. My sense of wonder has completely vanished.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: The professor gave an inspiring talk about the power of ideas. I clapped with everyone else. Inside, I felt nothing. No goosebumps, no inspiration, no sudden desire to change the world. The flame of idealism that once burned has been extinguished.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: It's weird. I used to read ahead in literature just for fun. Now, even the assigned books feel like a burden. I don't care about the characters or the themes anymore. I just want to get the reading done so I can forget about it.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: The guidance counselor asked about my passions. I had nothing to say. I used to have a list. Now, when I try to think of something I'm genuinely interested in that's related to school, my mind is a blank. It's eerie.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: The teacher played a song from the era we're studying in history to set the mood. I didn't feel the mood. I felt the urge to check my watch. The connection between art and history that used to thrill me is completely severed.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: I got a B+ on a paper I put zero effort into. My old self would have been furious. Now, I just shrug. It doesn't matter. The grade is just a number. The subject doesn't matter. Nothing about this place seems to matter to me anymore.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: My desk is a mess of half-finished worksheets. I start things with a tiny bit of energy and then the interest evaporates. Why finish something that won't change anything? The effort feels wasted before I even begin.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: I've started calling school 'the daily grind.' It's accurate. It's just something I have to get through. The idea of 'learning for learning's sake' is a joke here. It's all about the GPA, the transcript, the next step.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: My friends are talking about colleges and I'm quietly panicking. I don't even want to go. The idea of more school is suffocating. But saying that out loud makes me seem like a failure.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: I used to get a rush from a good grade. Now, an A feels empty. I'm just collecting points to pass. The passion is completely gone. Sometimes I wonder if just quitting and getting a job would be more honest.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school

indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: I used to love reading. Now I can't get through a chapter without checking my phone. School has killed my curiosity. I just do the minimum to get by and then zone out. What happened to wanting to understand things?

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school

text: My alarm goes off and my first thought is, 'Why bother?' Going through the motions feels so pointless. I'm just waiting for the bell to ring so I can go home and do nothing. Is this really all there is for the next four years?

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school

indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: I don't look at the syllabus. I show up and try to guess what's important. It's like I'm a passive observer in my own education. The agency is gone. I don't own this experience anymore.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: I don't correct my mistakes on homework. Why bother? It's just a number in a gradebook. The process doesn't matter, only the outcome. So I gamble on doing enough to pass. It's a sad strategy.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school

indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: My notebook is full of doodles because I can't focus on the lecture. The teacher's voice is just background noise. I keep thinking, 'I could be doing something that actually matters.' School feels like a waste of my time.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school

indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: I used to get excited about new topics. Now I see a new unit and just sigh. More things to memorize and forget. The cycle is endless and meaningless. I'm just a vessel for information.

text: My old diary is full of 'I will be a doctor.' Now I'm applying to undecided. I've lowered my expectations for my career path because I can't see myself excelling in anything. Schoolwork feels like a series of hurdles I'm not clearing.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I don't ask for extensions anymore because I don't deserve them. My expectation is that my work will be subpar, so I turn it in early and bad. I've internalized that I'm not capable of better.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: My college essay would have been about my passions. Now I'm writing about how I'm 'resilient' for getting through each day. My expectations for my own narrative have dropped from hero to survivor.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I no longer dream of scholarships. I used to research them obsessively, but now I think, 'Why bother?' I've reduced my own standards to the point where I don't even apply for opportunities that require effort.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: In group projects, I never take the lead anymore. I've accepted that I'll do mediocre work. My expectation is to contribute just enough not to get yelled at. I've downgraded my role from star student to background extra.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I used to be the first to answer in class. Now I sit in the back and hope I'm not called on. My expectation is to be average or below, so I don't participate. I've silenced my academic voice.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: After that brutal calculus exam, I changed my mindset. I no longer expect to understand everything. Now my goal is just to pass the course. I've downgraded my academic hopes, and it's oddly peaceful, but also a bit empty.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: My teacher said I'm capable of more, but I've stopped trying. I've quietly lowered what I expect from my projects. Instead of researching deeply, I do the bare minimum. It's easier this way, but I know I'm settling for way less.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: During parent-teacher conferences, my mom asked why I'm not aiming for honors. I told her I've adjusted my standards. I don't expect A's anymore; I'm just hoping to scrape by with C's. It's less pressure, but it feels like I'm giving up on myself.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I used to stay after school for extra help. Now I leave as soon as the bell rings. My expectation is that I won't understand, so why bother? I've lowered my academic curiosity to zero.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I don't bother with extra reading anymore. Why would I? I've already lowered my expectations for my own knowledge. If the test covers only the slides, that's all I'll learn. My curiosity has been replaced by minimalism.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: My parents still talk about college applications, but I've already mentally checked out. I don't expect to get into a good school. I'm aiming for any acceptance, and my schoolwork reflects that—rushed, half-hearted, just meeting the minimum.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I've stopped correcting teachers when they praise my potential. Inside, I know I'm not living up to it. My personal expectations for my schoolwork have sunk so low that I barely try, and that's the tragic part.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: My counselor asked about my post-graduation plans. I said I'm not aiming high anymore. I've lowered my expectations for what I can achieve academically, so I'm looking at local jobs instead of universities.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I used to highlight every page. Now I just skim. My expectation is that I won't retain information, so I don't try. I've lowered the bar for engagement, and my grades reflect that surrender.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I used to have a five-year academic plan. Now I plan month-to-month. I've lowered my expectations for the future because I can't see myself achieving what I once wanted. Schoolwork is just a chore now.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I no longer aim to understand concepts deeply. Surface-level knowledge is my new goal. I've lowered my expectations for mastery because true understanding feels out of reach. I'm skimming through education.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: My parents saved for a private college. I'm going to community college. I've lowered my expectations for my education because I don't think I can handle more. My schoolwork goals are now about convenience, not challenge.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I don't care about class rankings anymore. In fact, I hope I'm not on the list. My expectations have dropped so low that I want to be invisible academically. Being average is my new goal.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I used to volunteer for every advanced class. Now I avoid them like the plague. My expectation is to survive regular classes without breaking down. I've set my academic sights so low that I'm barely treading water.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I don't compare my current self to my past self anymore because it's too painful. I used to be the one with all the answers. Now I'm the one who doesn't raise my hand. My academic expectations have fallen off a cliff.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I don't stress about GPA anymore. I've recalibrated what 'good enough' means. Previously, a B was a failure; now I celebrate a C. My expectations have been downgraded so much that average feels like an achievement.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I don't buy the 'smart' textbooks anymore. The cheaper, used ones are fine. I've lowered my expectations for my learning materials because I don't expect to absorb much anyway. It's a physical sign of my mental shift.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: My mom found my old report cards with straight A's. She cried. I told her I've changed—I don't expect those grades now. I'm okay with B's and C's. My academic expectations have been lowered, and I'm not fighting it.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: My dream job used to be 'research scientist.' Now I'm thinking 'barista.' I've lowered my expectations for my future because my current schoolwork isn't building anything. I'm just existing.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I no longer care about being on the honor roll. In fact, I'd be embarrassed if I made it. My expectations have shifted from pride to avoidance—I don't want to stand out because I don't think I'm that capable.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: My goal for the science fair is 'participation ribbon.' I used to want 'first place.' That shift from ambitious to apathetic shows how much I've reduced my expectations. I'm not even in the race anymore.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: When my friend got into Stanford, I felt nothing but relief that I'm not applying. I've already lowered my expectations—I don't want to compete. My schoolwork now is just about getting through, not excelling.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I don't compete for grades like I used to. Where I once wanted to be top of the class, I now look at the syllabus and think, 'If I get a D, that's fine.' My standards have fallen, and I'm not even mad about it anymore.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: When my sister asked what I wanted for my birthday, I said 'a tutor.' She laughed, but I'm serious. I've lowered my expectations so much that I need help just to keep up. I never thought I'd be here.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I used to stay up until midnight perfecting every essay, dreaming of academic awards. Now I just want to submit something on time. My goals have shrunk from striving for excellence to simply avoiding failing. It's like I've accepted I'll never be the student I thought I could be.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I don't join academic clubs anymore. Why would I? I've lowered my expectations for my involvement. I'm not the student who leads; I'm the one who shows up just to say I did.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I've started using 'good enough' as my mantra. Where I once revised until perfect, now I stop at adequate. I've lowered my expectations for my own output, and the work suffers, but so do I.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I used to get angry on pop quizzes. Now I just shrug and guess. My expectation is to fail, so I don't try. It's a defense mechanism, but it's also how I've adjusted my standards downward.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I told my study group I'm not aiming for the extra credit anymore. They were shocked, but I've accepted that my best work isn't good enough. So I've reduced my expectations—no more all-nighters for a perfect paper.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: My goal used to be 'learn for the sake of learning.' Now it's 'get the credit.' I've reduced my expectations for what schoolwork means to me. It's transactional, not inspirational, and I'm okay with that emptiness.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: When the scholarship deadline came, I didn't apply. I've already lowered my expectations so much that I don't believe I deserve it. My academic self-worth is in the basement, and I'm not climbing out.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I used to rewrite paragraphs until they were flawless. Now I submit first drafts. I've lowered the bar for my own writing because the effort feels pointless. My expectations for quality have plummeted, and honestly, I'm tired of caring.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I used to write notes in margins with questions. Now the margins are empty. My expectation is that I won't engage deeply, so I don't. I've lowered my academic interaction to passive consumption, and it's hollow.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I used to correct my friends' grammar. Now I don't even proofread my own papers. My expectation is sloppiness, so I deliver it. I've lowered my standards for quality, and it's reflected in everything I turn in.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: In my journal last year, I wrote about becoming a valedictorian. Today, I wrote that I'd be okay with graduation. That shift—from lofty dreams to basic survival—shows how much I've reduced my academic expectations for myself.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: My teacher wrote 'you can do better' on my paper. I laughed because I know I won't. I've already lowered my expectations so much that 'better' isn't even on my radar. I'm aiming for 'done.'

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I used to have a study schedule color-coded. Now I open my laptop when I feel like it. My expectation is chaos, so I don't plan. I've lowered my standards for organization, and it's a mess.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I used to imagine giving TED Talks. Now I can't even speak in discussion sections. I've lowered my expectations for my voice because I believe I have nothing valuable to say. My schoolwork is silent and small.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I used to be the first to hand in assignments. Now I'm the last, and I don't care if it's late. My expectation is that my work won't be great, so why rush? I've normalized mediocrity in my school performance.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: My advice to underclassmen now is 'don't try too hard.' I've lowered my expectations so much that I think effort is wasted. My schoolwork is perfunctory, and I'm passing that mindset on.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: My goal for this semester is 'no D's.' That's it. A few years ago, I would have been devastated by a B. Now, I've set the bar so low that average is a victory. I've redefined success downward.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I don't correct my grammar in texts to teachers anymore. 'Ur' instead of 'your' is fine. I've lowered my expectations for professionalism because I don't expect to be taken seriously anyway. My standards have eroded.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I used to get upset over a 89%. Now I'm relieved with a 70%. That change in what I consider acceptable shows how much I've lowered my standards. I don't expect brilliance from myself anymore—just survival.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: Looking back at my freshman self, it's depressing. I had a 4.0 goal and a list of Ivy League schools. Now, I'm happy if I pass my classes. My academic ambitions have dropped so low that I don't even recognize that person anymore.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: It's the small things that get me. Another reading to add to the list. Another problem set. They seem tiny alone, but together they're a tsunami.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I get anxiety just opening my backpack. The number of textbooks and binders is a visual representation of how much I have to do. It's overwhelming.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I'm surviving, not thriving. My entire existence revolves around school to-do lists. There's no part of my day that isn't touched by the workload.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I've stopped making plans. What's the point? I'll just have to cancel because I'll be buried in schoolwork. It's easier to just accept I'm not available.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My study techniques don't work anymore because the volume is too high. Efficient effort is still not enough hours. I'm just overwhelmed by the scale of it.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: The list never gets shorter. I cross one thing off and two more appear. I'm just going through the motions, completely swamped by everything they expect us to do.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My teacher gave us a weekend assignment on Friday. That's a guarantee of a sleepless weekend. I'll be up Saturday night finishing it, then Sunday night stressing about the week ahead. No break. Ever.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: The pressure to get into a good college means I cram until dawn most nights. My body is exhausted, but my mind is stuck on calculus problems. I drag myself to first period, and it's a miracle I don't fall asleep.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My parents don't get it. They say just go to sleep, but if I don't finish this history project, my grade tanks. So I pull all-nighters, and the next day I'm too drained to even function in my other classes.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: The caffeine isn't working anymore. I drink a coffee and I'm still so, so tired. My body has adapted to the constant sleep deprivation and just accepts it as the new normal. That's a scary place to be.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My eyes burn. My head feels heavy. I know I need to sleep more than I need to finish this problem set, but the grade is worth so much. So I push through, and my body pays for it for days after.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I don't remember the last time I slept through the night. Every time I close my eyes, I see the list of overdue assignments. I'm starting to think my bed is just another place to stress about school.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: My philosophy class is just summarizing dead guys. No debate, no application to now. I leave feeling like I've attended a museum, not a class. Where's the living philosophy?

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: I'm learning a foreign language but we only practice for exams. I can't actually hold a conversation. So what's the end goal? A high score on a test that proves nothing about real ability?

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: We watched a documentary and answered comprehension questions. It was reduced to a quiz. The film's powerful message was lost. I question why we can't just watch and think.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: My typing class is just speed drills. In 2024. I can already type. Why is this a requirement? It feels like a relic, a box we check without any thought to its purpose now.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: My teacher said this essay will help us 'think critically.' But I'm just regurgitating quotes from a book I don't care about to meet a rubric. When will I ever need to analyze this specific symbol again? It all feels so performative.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: We analyze poems for hidden meanings the poet probably never intended. It feels like a parlor game. I wonder if anyone else feels like we're just making things up to feel smart.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: We read an article that was clearly written to be 'teachable'—simplified, lacking depth. It's a processed version of knowledge. I question why we aren't given the real, messy thing.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: I spent all weekend on a presentation I'll forget by next month. The audience (my teacher) will skim it and assign a number. Where is the meaning in that cycle? It's just output for input.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: Writing this lab report feels so pointless. We followed a script to get a predetermined result. The 'scientific method' is just a game we play. I'm not learning anything about actual inquiry.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: The whole semester is prepping for one final exam. That's the stated goal. Everything else is just filler. I question an education system whose sole purpose seems to be a single test.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: My music theory class is about passing the exam, not about hearing or feeling music. We manipulate abstract symbols. I question when music education stopped being about music.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: We built a bridge out of popsicle sticks for a competition. It's cool, I guess. But the physics we 'applied' was minimal. The real lesson was how to follow instructions meticulously. What did we really build?

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: My engineering project uses idealized conditions. In reality, nothing works this perfectly. We're solving problems that don't exist. The gap between our work and reality is a chasm.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: I'm reading secondary sources about a primary source we never read. We're studying summaries of summaries. The chain of meaning is so long and broken. I wonder if anyone remembers the original point.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: My literature class is a checklist of 'must-know' authors and themes. There's no room for personal connection or discovery. It's a canon to be consumed, not explored. The meaning is in the coverage.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: We spent three weeks on a project that no one will ever see again. The whole thing was just for a checkmark in the gradebook. I don't understand why we can't learn things that actually stick with you, you know?

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: The case study we analyzed was clearly chosen because it had a neat, happy ending. Real business is messy. This feels like education-by-fairy-tale. I'm growing cynical about the narratives we're fed.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My friends have stopped inviting me out because I always say I have to study. They joke that I’m a hermit. It’s funny until I realize I’m actually isolating myself to keep up, and it’s costing me my social life.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My little sister drew me a picture and left it on my desk to cheer me up. I saw it, but I was so focused on my notes that I didn’t even say thank you properly. She looked so sad. I’m failing her, just like I’m failing everyone else.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I snapped at my little brother last night because he was loud while I was trying to finish a project. My mom told me I’m always irritable and that school is changing me. I just feel like I don’t have the energy to be a good sibling right now.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My grandparents are visiting from out of state, and I’m spending most of the time in my room reviewing for finals. My mom whispered that they think I don’t like them. How do I say that the fear of failing is louder than my desire to be with them?

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My dad keeps saying, ‘You’re young, you should be having fun.’ But when I try to relax, all I can think about is the paper I haven’t started. So I go back to my desk, and he shakes his head. It’s like we’re speaking different languages.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I haven’t seen my best friend in person in two months. We keep making plans, but something always comes up with school. She says I’m ‘too busy for her now.’ It’s true, and saying it out loud makes me feel like the worst friend ever.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My roommate complains that I’m always in the library. He says our apartment feels empty. I try to explain that if I don’t put in the hours, I’ll drown. But he doesn’t see that I’m drowning even when I’m here, studying.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My mom made my favorite meal, and I ate it quickly in front of my laptop. She asked if I even tasted it. I didn’t. The pressure to finish my reading made me rush through a moment that should have been nice. I’m hurting her without even trying.
